Title: Water Deficit Increases Stilbene Metabolism in Cabernet Sauvignon Berries
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly induced after veraison through harvest in Cabernet Sauvignon under water deficit; reduced in Chardonnay under water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: one week after veraison through five weeks after veraison, transcript increased up to 10-fold in Cabernet Sauvignon under water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native STS promoter; cultivar-specific promoter polymorphisms analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: field water-deficit treatment; Affymetrix microarray transcript profiling; qPCR validation cited; HPLC-MS metabolite profiling; promoter sequencing/computational cis-element analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes trans-piceid accumulation under water deficit in Cabernet Sauvignon
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STS was the most strongly water-deficit-induced resveratrol-pathway gene in Cabernet Sauvignon berries. 2. Its transcript abundance increased about 10-fold after veraison under water deficit, whereas it declined in Chardonnay. 3. Increased STS expression, but not glycosyltransferase expression, was associated with a 5-fold increase in trans-piceid. 4. Promoter polymorphisms suggest cultivar-specific transcriptional control of STS.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; supported as regulatory/decisive pathway step for trans-piceid accumulation in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Seven additional STS isogenes showed similar trends but were not statistically significant; 12 SNPs in the proximal promoter generated eight cis-element changes between cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value target for metabolic engineering or marker-assisted selection to enhance stilbene nutraceutical content under deficit irrigation; promoter alleles may be useful
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nutraceutical precursor of resveratrol in humans after hydrolysis; may contribute to pathogen resistance in grape berries

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: activates 4-coumarate to 4-coumaroyl-CoA, the precursor for trans-resveratrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: increased after veraison through harvest in Cabernet Sauvignon under water deficit; decreased in Chardonnay under water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Affymetrix microarray transcript profiling under field water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports precursor formation for increased stilbene biosynthesis in Cabernet Sauvignon under water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL transcript abundance increased significantly in water-deficit-treated Cabernet Sauvignon berries after veraison. 2. The same transcript decreased in Chardonnay under water deficit. 3. The gene likely contributes to coumaroyl-CoA supply for resveratrol/stilbene formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; discussed as potential regulatory role in precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper cites heterologous expression evidence from tobacco 4CL enhancing resveratrol biosynthesis, but this is background support not direct validation in grape

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates cinnamate to p-coumarate in the phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: increased from veraison through harvest in Cabernet Sauvignon under water deficit; not affected in Chardonnay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Affymetrix microarray transcript profiling under field water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with increased precursor flux toward stilbene synthesis in Cabernet Sauvignon
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. One C4H transcript increased in abundance under water deficit in Cabernet Sauvignon from veraison through harvest. 2. The same gene was not affected in Chardonnay. 3. The response is consistent with enhanced phenylpropanoid precursor formation for stilbenes in Cabernet Sauvignon.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate, initiating phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; first committed step of phenylpropanoid entry
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: coordinately increased from veraison through harvest in Cabernet Sauvignon under water deficit; reduced in Chardonnay under water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Affymetrix microarray transcript profiling under field water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with enhanced upstream precursor production for stilbene accumulation in Cabernet Sauvignon
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A PAL unigene showed cultivar-specific transcriptional responses to water deficit. 2. Transcript abundance increased in Cabernet Sauvignon but decreased in Chardonnay under water deficit. 3. The response suggests enhanced phenylpropanoid entry into stilbene biosynthesis in Cabernet Sauvignon.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established for target metabolite in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes homology to Arabidopsis AtPAL2 associated with environmentally triggered flavonoid synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transcript abundance declined during development and was unaffected by water deficit in both cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Affymetrix microarray transcript profiling; HPLC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: not responsive to water deficit; does not explain increased trans-piceid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The putative resveratrol-O-glucosyltransferase transcript declined over development. 2. Water deficit did not affect its transcript abundance in either cultivar. 3. Therefore this step did not account for the increased trans-piceid in Cabernet Sauvignon.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not supported as key control step under water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors emphasize that although this gene is most directly related to piceid formation, its transcript was unchanged by water deficit
